_doc: Crosswalk that resolves every funnel-template + automation-template persona reference to a REAL canonical Skill-22 persona-categories.json id, so 'copy from the personas' always pulls a real persona-blueprint. Resolution order (most specific first): (1) the ref IS a canonical id; (2) exact slug_map hit; (3) ordered title/concept/author pattern scan. Validate with shared-utils/persona_crosswalk.py --validate. EVERY target below MUST exist in 22-book-to-persona-coaching-leadership-system/persona-categories.json (the validator enforces it).
_email_doc: email_persona_styles crosswalks Skill-50's 12 self-contained email tone-STYLE ids to REAL canonical persona ids, so an email selection joins the persona adherence/learning loop (F4.3). Only styles with a genuine canonical counterpart are mapped — a style with no library persona (e.g. Tony Robbins != the library's Mel Robbins; Iyanla/Les Brown/Lisa Nichols/Gladwell/Brené have no library persona) is intentionally OMITTED and keeps its own style-tier behavior. Every mapped target MUST exist in persona-categories.json (persona_crosswalk.py --validate enforces it alongside slug_map/patterns).
_copy_craft_pool_doc: D5/B-D1 (RATIFIED 2026-07-14) — KILLS the old 5-surname copy-persona cap. VOICE may be ANY of the 99 catalog personas (audience-led, the entire point of the blend). The copy-craft TASK slot (the persona whose craft DISCIPLINE governs how a copy task is executed, not who it sounds like) must instead come from this pool: the ORIGINAL five craft-discipline surnames (bly, wiebe, miller, hormozi, cialdini) resolved to their REAL canonical ids — expanded to every book variant a surname covers, since the old cap named bare surnames, not ids — PLUS every edwards-* id PLUS the full Brunson-family crosswalk targets (patterns above). Defined ONCE here; referenced by both v2-autonomous-build-sop.md (P2 step 2) and universal-sops/funnel-craft/SOP-FUNNEL-02-COPY.md (Step 0); machine-validated by persona_crosswalk.py --validate (every member MUST exist in persona-categories.json, exactly like slug_map/patterns targets).
